name: jsm-user-services

metadata.name: jsm-user-services
metadata.summary: Middleware to intercept JWT auth token and more utils functions

documentation:
# JSM User JWT Service

Middleware to intercept JWT auth token and more utils functions

## Local development

### Running tests

`docker-compose up integration-tests`

### Running lint and code formatter

`docker-compose up lint-formatter`

## Install - For Django users

`pip install jsm-user-services[drf]`

Add `jsm_user_services` to your `INSTALLED_APPS`:

```python
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    "django.contrib.admin",
    "django.contrib.auth",
    "django.contrib.contenttypes",
    "django.contrib.sessions",
    "django.contrib.messages",
    "django.contrib.staticfiles",
    "jsm_user_services",
    "app_test",
]
```

Add the Middleware:

```python
MIDDLEWARE = [
    ...
    "jsm_user_services.middleware.JsmJwtService",
]

# Dev url
USER_API_HOST = "http://ishtar-gate.dev.juntossomosmaisi.com.br/api/v1"
USER_API_TOKEN = "user_api_token"
```

## Install - For Flask users

`pip install jsm-user-services[flask]`

Add the middleware in your Flask app:

```python
from flask import Flask
from jsm_user_services.flask_module import middleware

app = Flask(__name__)

middleware.JsmJwtService(app)
app.config.update(
    USER_API_HOST="http://ishtar-gate.dev.juntossomosmaisi.com.br/api/v1", USER_API_TOKEN="user_api_token"
)
```

**DISCLAIMER**
- Currently, only the middleware is implemented for Flask apps

## Use

```python
from jsm_user_services.services.user import current_jwt_token
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_jsm_token
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_jsm_user_data_from_jwt
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_ltm_token
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_user_email_from_jwt
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_user_id_from_jwt
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_user_data_from_server
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_user_data_from_cpf
from jsm_user_services.services.user import get_cpf_from_jwt

current_jwt_token()
"""
Response example:
"eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..oZvlK-XOBrgN9xZrChHHRdQ0rLMFTPp9jjuGvGM1U78"
"""

get_jsm_token()
"""
Response example:
"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..4dT-6_PmJEbWGw5Q1F18mJBQcXcVmNugQwiLxp6e4Ew"
"""
get_ltm_token()
"""
```

## Versioning
This lib follows the [pypi version format](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/) with the convention of using 
_major_._minor_._patch_ version.

### When to bump a patch version?
Bump the patch version if you are doing a quick fix, nothing that changes the library functionality.

### When to bump the minor version?
Bump the minor version if you are adding new functionality without breaking backwards compatibility. For example, 
adding support to new events.

### When to bump the major version?
Bump the major version if you are breaking backwards compatibility by adding new functionality or refactoring.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.flask_module.middleware.JsmJwtService.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the JsmJwtService middleware if the flask application (`app`) is given. Otherwise, you'll
have the option to initialize it later.

Examples:
- Initialize as soon as possible:

```
from flask import Flask
from jsm_user_services.flask_module.middleware import JsmJwtService

app = Flask(__name__)
JsmJwtService(app)
```

- Postpone initialization:

```
from flask import Flask
from jsm_user_services.flask_module.middleware import JsmJwtService

app, middleware = Flask(__name__), JsmJwtService()
middleware.init_app(app)
```

methods.name: jsm_user_services.flask_module.middleware.JsmJwtService._get_jwt_token_from_request
documentation: Extracts JWT token from a Flask request object.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.flask_module.middleware.JsmJwtService._get_user_ip_from_request
documentation:
Retrieve the user ip that made this request from Flask request object

When running a service behind Akamai or other CDN solutions, it is expected that this header might contain a
string with multiple IPs (comma separated values).
In this case, the user's public IP that originated the request is considered to be the first one of this list.
For reference: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/X-Forwarded-For

modules.name: jsm_user_services.support.http_utils
documentation: HTTP utils module

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.http_utils.request
documentation:
Generate a requests session that allows retries and raises HTTP errors (status code >= 400).
Uses the same arguments as the class Retry from urllib3

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.http_utils.get_response_body
documentation: Deserialize response

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.http_utils.convert_header_to_meta_key
documentation:
Django Http Request has a dictionary called META with the request headers.
But it replaces the header name following the rules:
  - all characters to uppercase
  - replacing any hyphens with underscores
  - adding an HTTP_ prefix to the name.
So, for example, a header called X-Bender would be mapped to the META key HTTP_X_BENDER.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.email_utils.validate_format_email
documentation: Check if the given string has a valid format e-mail

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.email_utils.perform_callback_function_validators
documentation:
Returns if the e-mail is valid by `functions_to_validate_email`
    Parameters:
        `functions_to_validate_email (List[Callable])`: a list of functions to validate the e-mail
        `email (str)`: the e-mail that will be validated

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.auth_jwt.get_decoded_jwt_token
documentation: Gets a decoded JWT token with the HS256 algorithm.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.auth_jwt.get_decoded_oauth_token
documentation: Gets a decoded JWT token with the RS256 algorithm.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.auth_jwt.get_bearer_authorization_token
documentation:
Retrieve a bearer authorization token from an Authorization header value.

It expects the header value to be something on the lines of: "Bearer token".

Examples:
- get_bearer_authorization_token("Bearer token") # returns "token"
- get_bearer_authorization_token("bearer token") # returns None
- get_bearer_authorization_token("Token token") # returns None
- get_bearer_authorization_token("whatever") # returns None

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.settings_utils.get_from_settings_or_raise_missing_config
documentation: Tries to retrieve the value from attribute in settings. If it was not possible to retrieve the value, raises.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.support.request_id.get_request_id_header_name
documentation:
Helper function to get request_id header name to included in request to another application
REQUEST_ID_CONFIG dict configuration exists in the projects with similarity structure below
REQUEST_ID_CONFIG = {
    "REQUEST_ID_HEADER": "HTTP_X_REQUEST_ID",
    "GENERATE_REQUEST_ID_IF_NOT_FOUND": True,
    "RESPONSE_HEADER_REQUEST_ID": "HTTP_X_REQUEST_ID",
}

functions.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.helpers.is_exception_related_to_api_exception

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.helpers.AllowClassBehaviorAsFunction

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.helpers.AllowClassBehaviorAsFunction.__call__
documentation:
The __call__ method enables classes to be writen where the instances behave like functions and can be called
like a function. When the instance is called as a function; if this method is defined,
x(arg1, arg2, ...) is a shorthand for x.__call__(arg1, arg2, ...).

Google Recaptcha Permission Example:
This method allows to use this Permission Class with a custom response without being forced to use
"get_permissions".

Without this method, is necessary to do something like this:
    def get_permissions(self) -> List:
        exc = SomeException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, {"details": "Some message"})
        return [GoogleRecaptchaPermission(exc)]

With this method, one can achieve the same result using something like this:
    permission_classes = [
        GoogleRecaptchaPermission(SomeException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, {"details": "Some message"}))
    ]

This class should be used when the inherited class instance should be 'called' as a function.
It's very useful when it's necessary to initiate a PermissionClass and then add it on `permission_class`.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.exception.ApplicationSettingsNotFound
documentation: Exception that indicates the application settings was not found through known methods.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.exception.RequestIDModuleNotFound
documentation: Exception that indicates the request id module was not found through known methods.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.exception.DecoratorWrongUsage
documentation: Generic exception when a decorator is used wrongly.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.exception.EmailValidationError
documentation: Exception that indicates Everest is down and the default validation methods will not be used.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.google.perform_recaptcha_validation
documentation:
Performs a request to Google in order to validate the reCAPTCHA.
For more details, check: https://developers.google.com/recaptcha/docs/verify

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.everest.perform_email_validation
documentation:
Parameters:
    `email (str)`: the e-mail that will be validated
    `use_callback_function_validator (bool)`: indicates if some validators must be considered if the `status_code`
    is different from 200 (success)
    `functions_to_validate_email (List[Callable])`: list of validators that will be considered if the `status_code`
    is different from 200 (success) and the `use_callback_function_validator` is `False`

Returns:
    A dict containing two values:
    - `is_valid (bool)`: indicates if the email string is valid
    - `external_api_validation (bool)`: indicates if the email validation was made by Everest or locally

Response success example:
    {
        "meta": {},
        "results": {
            "category": "valid",
            "status": "valid",
            "name": "Valid",
            "definition": "The email has a valid account associated with it.",
            "reasons": [],
            "risk": "low",
            "recommendation": "send",
            "address": "ayron41@gmail.com",
            "diagnostics": {
                "role_address": false,
                "disposable": false,
                "typo": false
            }
        }
    }

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.everest.get_email_validation
documentation: Returns the payload from Everest

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.user.jwt_has_required_roles
documentation: Asserts that the jwt token has all the required roles.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.user.get_jwt_algorithm
documentation: Retrieves the algorithm used in the JWT token.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.user.get_oauth_token
documentation:
This function retrieves the OAuth token from the local threading.
If the token is not present, it returns None.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.user.get_user_id_auth
documentation:
This function retrieves the user's ID from an OAuth JWT token.
If the "sub" field is not present, it returns None.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.user.get_user_email_from_oauth_jwt
documentation:
This function retrieves the user's email from an OAuth JWT token.
If the "email" field is not present, it returns None.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.services.user.get_user_id_auth_or_user_id_ref_in_jwt
documentation: Retrieves the user_id_auth or user_id_ref from the JWT token according to the algorithm used.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.middleware.JsmJwtService._get_jwt_token_from_request
documentation: Extracts JWT token from a Django request object.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.middleware.JsmJwtService._get_user_ip_from_request
documentation:
Retrieve the user ip that made this request from Django HttpRequest object

When running a service behind Akamai or other CDN solutions, it is expected that this header might contain
a string with multiple IPs (comma separated values).
In this case, the user's public IP that originated the request is considered to be the first one of this list.
For reference: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/X-Forwarded-For

modules.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions
documentation: Module with useful user permissions classes meant to be used with Django Rest Framework.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.JSMUserBasePermission

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.JSMUserBasePermission._validate_request_against_user_service
documentation: Gets valid user_data from the User micro service.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.JSMUserBasePermission.has_permission
documentation: Abstract method that must be implemented by children classes.

Base class for JSM user permissions. Implements methods for validating requests against
the user micro service so that any type of permission can be carried out (role, status, etc).

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.StatusBasedPermission

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.StatusBasedPermission._validate_user_status
documentation: Validates if the user has the appropriate status against the allowed status.

Base class for the status-based permissions. Implements methods for validating requests against
the user micro service and an utility method that asserts that the user has the appropriate status.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.RoleBasedPermission

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.RoleBasedPermission._validate_user_role
documentation: Validates if the user has the appropriate role against the allowed roles.

Base class for the role-based permissions. Implements methods for validating requests against
the user micro service and an utility method that asserts that the user has the appropriate role.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.AnyLoggedUserPermission.has_permission

Permission that allows users to access the requested resource regardless
of their roles as long as they have a VALID, NON EXPIRED JSM JWT.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.GoogleRecaptchaPermission

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.GoogleRecaptchaPermission.__init__
documentation:
Sets the default value for "exception_in_case_of_failed_verification" property. It will be used to return
a custom response, in case of this permission fails.
This exception must inherit APIException, from rest_framework.exceptions, otherwise it will be ignored.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.GoogleRecaptchaPermission.has_permission
documentation:
Retrieves the Google data on the request and then performs a request to Google, in order to check if the
received data is valid or not.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.GoogleRecaptchaPermission._retrieve_g_recaptcha_response
documentation:
Lookup on the request's body and header in order to retrieve the recaptcha key.

 If the same key is present on header and body, the header data will be overridden by the body value.
 Also, if both keys are present, the priority is "g_recaptcha_response" over "g-recaptcha-response".

A permission class which checks if the request is authorized by Google Recaptcha V3.

In order to use it, the request must have the key "g_recaptcha_response" or "g-recaptcha-response" on the request's
body or header. Otherwise the request won't be authorized without even checking it on Google.

Just be aware that a header with underscore is not allowed. So do not use the key "g_recaptcha_response"
on the header.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.IsUserAllowedToPermission

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.IsUserAllowedToPermission.__init__
documentation:
Sets the default value for "exception_in_case_of_failed_verification" property. It will be used to return
a custom response, in case of this permission fails. This exception must inherit APIException,
from rest_framework.exceptions, otherwise it will be ignored.

Full match property indicates that user data must contains all the 'not_allowed_to' defined parameters.
On the other hand, if full match is equal to False, the permission class checks
if user data contains any of the 'not_allowed_to' parameters.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.IsUserAllowedToPermission._verify_if_user_should_be_allowed
documentation: Validates 'not_allowed_to' user parameter against the 'not_allowed_actions' permission class parameter.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.IsUserAllowedToPermission.has_permission
documentation: Asserts that the user hasn't an action restriction against the 'not_allowed_to' permission class parameter.

A permission class which checks if user is authorized to execute an action based on 'not_allowed_to' parameter.
Implements methods for validating requests against the user micro service and an utility method that asserts
the user has the appropriate permission for to do, or not to do, a determined action.

Example:
    class SomeAwesomeView():
        permission_classes = [
            IsUserAllowedToPermission(
                ["redeem", "score"], full_match=False, exception_in_case_of_failed=CustomExceptionNotAllowedTo
            )
        ]

    In this example, the custom exception should be implemented into your own project.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.JuntosIdUserIsAuthenticatedInLOPermission
documentation: Permission to check if the user is authenticated with an Oauth or Loyalty JWT.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.drf_tools.permissions.OauthUserPermission
documentation: Permission to check if the user is authenticated with an Oauth JWT.

functions.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd.anonymize_sensible_data
jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.traverse_within_dict_overriding_data
documentation: Method that navigates through the dict and modifies the necessary keys.

classes.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic

methods.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic.anonymize_name
documentation:
Anonymize strategy for "name" type. It uses the following logic:
\>>> anonymize_name("Lucas Gigek Carvalho")

> Lucas C

In order to do it, the name must be a string.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic.anonymize_cpf
documentation:
Anonymize strategy for "CPF" type. It uses the following logic:
\>>> anonymize_cpf("12345678910")

> 8910

In order to do it, the cpf must be a string.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic.anonymize_phone
documentation:
Anonymize strategy for "phone" type. It uses the following logic:
\>>> anonymize_phone("5511912345678")

> 5678

In order to do it, the phone must be a string.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic.anonymize_email
documentation:
Anonymize strategy for "email" type. It uses the following logic:
\>>> anonymize_email("<mailto:lucas.carvalho@juntossomosmais.com.br>")

> <mailto:l*************@juntossomosmais.com.br>

In order to do it, the email must be a string.

methods.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic.anonymize_address_should_not_be_returned
documentation:
Anonymize strategy for "address" type which shouldn't be returned. It uses the following logic:
\>>> anonymize_address_should_not_be_returned("Av. Gomes de Carvalho")

> None

methods.name: jsm_user_services.decorators.lgpd_utils.AnonymizeLogic.anonymize_address_should_be_returned
documentation:
Anonymize strategy for "address" type which should be returned. It uses the following logic:
\>>> anonymize_address_should_be_returned("São Paulo")

> "São Paulo"
An Enum that allows decorator's user decide which logic should be used.
